other_observers: Susan Allerton for both sightings. Barb Byron was also present for the May 10th sighting.; Susan and I were the original observers for the April 20th sighting. Susan Allerton was the original observer for the May 10th sighting, which was a different bird.
specific_location: Eastern edge of Pastorius Reservoir south-east of Durango off CR 304 east of CR 301.
description: Bird was a medium large wader with a long decurved bill. It was with a sizable flock of about thirty plegadis waders. This bird did not have any white feathering around the facial skin and the facial skin was a dark grayish-blue bordered by a paler gray-blue edge both above and below the facial skin at the base of the bill and behind the gape. The iris was dark without any sign of red. The bill was brownish-gray with the tip being more grayish. The body was a glossy blackish reddish brown and the wings were dark with glossy greenish highlights. The legs were a dark brown except the knees were reddish. This individual was slightly larger than average for this group of plegadis waders.Behaviors: It was associating with a flock of White-faced Ibis and feeding at the shore-line of Pastorius Reservoir.Call: Not heardPlumage: Alternate
